classes.reasoning: This is a published review article from the Indian Heart Journal (volume 76, 2024, pages S86-S89) with standard journal formatting including DOI, publication dates (received, revised, accepted), author affiliations, abstract, keywords, and extensive bibliography. It is a peer-reviewed medical literature review on dyslipidemia and peripheral arterial disease, not a clinical trial artifact.

intervention: Lipid-lowering therapies including statins, ezetimibe, PCSK9 inhibitors (evolocumab, alirocumab), fibrates (fenofibrate, gemfibrozil, pemafibrate), icosapent ethyl, bempedoic acid, and inclisiran
summary: This is a published medical review article from the Indian Heart Journal examining the relationship between dyslipidemia and peripheral arterial disease (PAD). The article reviews current guidelines for lipid management, discusses the evidence base for various lipid-lowering therapies (statins, ezetimibe, PCSK9 inhibitors, fibrates, and other agents) in PAD patients, and calls for Indian-specific research on the impact of lipid-lowering agents on limb salvage rates in PAD populations.
references_to: 2019 ECS/EAS guidelines for the management of dyslipidaemias
REACH (Reduction of Atherothrombosis for Continued Health) registry
FOURIER TRIAL (Further Cardiovascular Outcomes Research with PCSK9 Inhibition in Subjects with Elevated Risk)
PROSPER (Prospective Study of Pravastatin in the Elderly at Risk)
FIELD study (Fenofibrate Intervention and Event Lowering in Diabetes)
REDUCE-IT trial (Reduction of Cardiovascular Events with Icosapent Ethyl-Intervention Trial)
CLEAR outcomes trial (Bempedoic acid and cardiovascular outcomes in statin intolerant patients)
